name: Cajun Prime Rib
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the fat cap from the top of the meat (butcher can do this for you) and save.
2. Place the roast, standing on the rib bones, in a very large roasting pan.
3. Make several punctures through the silver skin with a knife so seasoning can permeate the meat.
4. Pour a generous, even layer of black pepper over the top of the meat (the pepper should completely cover it). Repeat with garlic powder and then salt, totally covering the preceding layers.
5. Carefully arrange the onions in an even layer on top without knocking off the seasoning.
6. Place the fat cap back on top. Refrigerate for 24 hours.
7. Bake ribs in a 550°F oven until the fat is dark brown and crispy on top, about 35 minutes.
8. Remove from oven and let cool slightly. Refrigerate until well chilled, about 3 hours (this solidifies the juices and allows the steaks to be cooked rare).
9. Remove the fat cap and discard. Scrape off the onions and as much seasoning as possible with a large knife; then discard.
10. Slice between ribs into 6 steaks (4 will have bones). Trim the cooked surface of meat from the two pieces that were on the outside of the roast.
11. Season and cook steaks in your favorite way.
12. To blacken the steaks: Combine seasoning mix ingredients thoroughly in a small bowl; you'll have about 8 tablespoons. Sprinkle generously and evenly on both sides of each steak, using about 4 teaspoons per steak, and press with hands.
13. Heat a cast iron skillet over very high heat until it is smoking and white ash forms on the bottom for at least 10 minutes (do not let the skillet get too hot).
14. Place one steak in the hot skillet; cook only one side at a time. Cook over very high heat until a heavy black crust develops, about 2 to 3 minutes.
15. Turn the steak and cook until the underside is similarly crusted for about 2 to 3 more minutes.
16. Repeat with remaining steaks.
17. Serve each steak while hot.
